feed.title: Current Watches, Warnings and Advisories for New Zealand issued by MetService
feed.rights: Copyright Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited, 2026. Licensed under Creative Commons BY 4.0.

entry.title: Heavy Rain Watch
entry.summary: Periods of heavy rain, and amounts may approach warning criteria about the ranges. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Strong Wind Watch
entry.summary: South to southeast winds may approach severe gale in exposed places. Impact: Damage to trees, powerlines, and unsecured structures possible. Driving may be difficult, especially for high-sided vehicles and motorcycles. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: High. Changes: Chance of upgrade to an Orange Warning has been increased to high.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Watch
entry.summary: A period of heavy rain, and amounts may approach warning criteria. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Watch
entry.summary: Periods of heavy rain, amounts may approach warning criteria. Further rain is possible later on Thursday. Impact: Streams and rivers may rise rapidly. Surface flooding, slips, and difficult driving conditions possible. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: High. Changes: Watch extended to include the eastern hills of Wellington.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Red
entry.summary: Expect 70 to 120 mm, on top of what has fallen. Peak rates of 10 to 20 mm/h likely, especially about the ranges. During this period, coastal locations may receive 30 to 60 mm of rain. Freezing level about 1600 metres and snow likely above 1200 metres. Note, further rain is expected through the remainder of Wednesday and into Thursday, and accumulation may approach or exceed warning amounts, and the Red Warning may be extended or downgraded to an Orange Rain Warning Wednesday morning. Impact: Threat to life from dangerous river conditions, significant flooding and slips. Conditions will disrupt travel, make some roads impassable, and isolate communities. Changes: Validity extended to 10am Wednesday.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Snow showers expected down to 900 metres at first and lowering to 600 metres from 10pm. Accumulations of 1-2 cm are possible about the Arthur's Pass summit and lesser amounts down to 600 metres.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Snow showers expected to develop Wednesday morning. 1 to 3 cm of snow may accumulate above 700 metres, with lesser amounts down to 500 metres.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Snow showers expected on Wednesday. Accumulations of 2-3cm are possible above 700 metres during this time with lesser amounts down to 500 metres.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Light snow showers are expected Wednesday morning to early afternoon. Expect 1 or 2 cm of snow above 600 metres.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Snow showers expected down to 900 metres at first and lowering to 600 metres from 10pm. Accumulations of 5-8 cm are likely near the summit with lesser amounts down to 600 metres.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited
